findings.summary: PAEChannelMixer 3x3 matrix multiply VERIFIED at 0.71 levels across identity/swap/luma601/half-mix on gray+saturated inputs. FCP does the channel-dot in sRGB code values, matching the engine exactly (e.g. red_luma601 0.3R+0.59G+0.11B on (200,50,50)->94.87 vs TS 95).
findings.significance: SECOND colour node VERIFIED (with PAELevels). SHARPENS the root cause decisively: a cross-channel LINEAR COMBINATION (matrix dot, incl. luma-weighted) is FAITHFUL in sRGB — so the colour divergence is NOT 'all cross-channel ops' and NOT linear-combinations. It is specific to: Brightness (multiply x amount + clip coupling), Colorize/Tint (luma->colour MIX + hard-light), HSV (saturation). The linear-working-space fix must target THOSE ops, not channel-mix or gamma or matrix dots (which are already exact in sRGB).
findings.map: FAITHFUL in sRGB: curve interp, blur decimation, per-channel gamma (Levels), channel matrix-dot (ChannelMixer), all darken/identity. DIVERGES (linear working space): brightness multiply-with-clip, luma->colour mix (Colorize), hard-light tint, HSV saturation/value.
